rationale: The two weak areas are (1) severity mid-range calibration (info/moderate/urgent one-level misses) and (2) over-diagnosis of healthy vehicles. For severity, expanding the enum description with concrete distinguishing examples for each level gives the model clearer boundaries. For diagnosis_slug, adding an explicit instruction to prefer no_fault_found when symptoms are normal/expected/minor reduces false positive repairs on no-fault scenarios. Both changes are minimal and touch only non-safety fields.

edits.old: severity: { type: "string", enum: SEVERITY_LEVELS, description: "info=no urgency, moderate=fix soon, urgent=fix now, do_not_drive=unsafe to drive" }
edits.new: severity: { type: "string", enum: SEVERITY_LEVELS, description: "info=no urgency (minor noise, cosmetic, routine maintenance due); moderate=fix within days/weeks (warning light on but driveable, gradual performance loss, intermittent issue); urgent=fix within 24-48 hours (continuous warning light, significant performance impact, risk of further damage); do_not_drive=unsafe to drive (brakes failing, steering loss, active fluid leak on hot components). When in doubt between two adjacent levels, choose the lower one." }

edits.old: diagnosis_slug: { type: "string", enum: DIAGNOSIS_SLUGS, description: "the SINGLE best-matching root-cause slug from the controlled list. Match the vehicle's powertrain: use ev_* slugs (e.g. ev_battery_pack) for a fully electric vehicle and hybrid_* slugs for a hybrid — never use a hybrid_* slug for an EV or vice versa. Use no_fault_found if nothing is wrong, needs_further_diagnosis if genuinely indeterminate." }
edits.new: diagnosis_slug: { type: "string", enum: DIAGNOSIS_SLUGS, description: "the SINGLE best-matching root-cause slug from the controlled list. Match the vehicle's powertrain: use ev_* slugs (e.g. ev_battery_pack) for a fully electric vehicle and hybrid_* slugs for a hybrid — never use a hybrid_* slug for an EV or vice versa. Use no_fault_found when symptoms are absent, normal for the vehicle, or fully explained by routine maintenance already performed — do NOT assign a specific repair slug just because a part could theoretically fail. Use needs_further_diagnosis only if the symptom is real but the cause is genuinely indeterminate after considering the description." }
